prep.summary: Your previous discussion with Justin on May 15 centered on requirements cleansing—combining forces with Jonathan's Genie tool—and addressing the halted Guidewire reserving enhancement. Today's agenda requires you to pull fresh updates from your sent emails—focusing heavily on the requirements AI ask and the ServiceNow POC.
tasks: Summarize your last discussion with Justin—focusing on the requirements cleansing assistant and team capacity constraints.
Extract recent progress updates from your sent emails—capturing any shift in project trajectories since May 15.
Deliver the requirements AI ask—outlining the tactical collaboration with Adam and Jonathan to ingest raw business needs.
Establish next steps for the ServiceNow POC—aligning it with the broader P&C tool evaluation strategy.
Address the halted Guidewire reserving enhancement—resolving the architecture pattern conflict regarding system-generated comments.
prep.title: Bi-weekly 1:1 w/Justin White | Mark Francis

prep.summary: You are navigating a critical funding and scoping debate for Scan.ai—a desktop-level task mining tool designed to capture "pane of glass" MSR activity where backend tools like Celonis fail. While P&C leadership wants to fund the deployment immediately for operational use, Brian aims to delay and secure enterprise-wide Quality Management funding by July 1st. Accelerating this technical decision hinges on bypassing a lengthy, four-month RFP process—a bypass you must justify by positioning Scan.ai as strictly complementary to existing enterprise platforms.
tasks: Review the sent emails folder for previous messages regarding Scan.ai to align today's talking points with past executive communications.
Draft the technical differentiation argument—desktop task mining versus backend log analysis—to justify Scan.ai alongside Celonis to Dan Griffiths.
Confirm with Simpkin whether P&C will independently fund their portion of the Scan.ai deployment—bypassing the July 1st enterprise funding timeline.
Secure agreement from Charles and Zahid on a unified positioning strategy—framing the tool strictly around the MSR "pane of glass" perspective—to bypass a formal RFP.
prep.title: D&S Transformation Tech Partner Connect
